segments.text: Yo, Corbin Braxton, what up, bro? I'm chilling man. Yeah. Hey, congrats on the big race. Like you guys are killing it
Hey, thanks, dude. Say, are you uh, you still your dad's thing? LARP or whatever. Yeah. Yeah logistics analytics and reporting partners
Yeah, right. Well, how's it going? It's been really good lately. Actually, I'm gonna be getting the full-time offer really soon
So, okay. Well, what are you doing on Verso jobs then? What on on what Verso, dude?
They're recruiting service. You were like a 90% match for our platform engineering role. What happened?
Oh, yeah, I mean, I filled out some stuff like a month ago, but I was just kind of looking around nothing serious
Okay, so are you looking or are you not? I mean
Low-key like I feel like just sticking it out here might be the move long term really
You've been an intern for what a year now
Uh, yeah 16 months, but i'm like a lock for getting promoted. So i'm chilling and you know that for sure
Yeah, bro. They literally told me if I can talk like them and disagree with everyone I can move up the ladder
It's cheese. No offense, dude, but that sounds like hell. No, dude. It's not that bad
I literally just got to sit in these meetings
I pull up gpt and it gives me some linkedin speak jargon to spit out and these guys eat it up every time
So you'd rather play theater with a bunch of corporate muppets and build something with your body that could actually change the world
I mean
For now like yeah, dude, I know it's honest work
But that just feels like kind of an unexciting path for you, you know
Well, maybe but I mean it worked for my dad, right? Yeah, no kidding. What's he making?
Uh, never mind. Uh, look dude, we got to fill this role fast
So if you change your mind, I can't promise we'll have anything for you. So are you getting out? Yeah, i'm out, man
Sorry, it's it's nothing personal. All right, dude. Good luck. Yeah, I wish you the best. Hey, thanks, bro
Good luck to you too, man. Thanks, man. Peace
